title: Here Comes Treble

jokes.text: This year I decided to really get into the spirit of Halloween.
jokes.setup: Dwight confidently declares his Halloween enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that he's trapped in a pumpkin
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overconfidence leading to absurd physical predicament. Visual is inherently funny and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am the master of Halloween. I have been preparing for this moment all year.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am a fearsome warrior, ready to conquer all who dare challenge me.

jokes.text: *** decision I've ever made.
jokes.setup: Following his enthusiastic Halloween declaration
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal - it's the worst decision
jokes.explanation: Simple reversal gag that's effective but not exceptional. Gets the job done.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Halloween is the best day of the year.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Actually, it's the worst decision I've ever made.

jokes.text: My *** head went into the pumpkin no problem... but I can't get it out.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how he got stuck
jokes.punchline: The absurd physics of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight logic - overconfident planning with no exit strategy. Physical absurdity sells it.

jokes.text: I mean, the pumpkin should rot off of my head in a month or two... right?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to rationalize his predicament
jokes.punchline: Casually accepting he'll wear a rotting pumpkin for months
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - treating a nightmare scenario as mildly inconvenient. The casual 'right?' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, I've accepted my fate. A rotting pumpkin on my head for months. It's actually quite practical—the decomposition will provide natural nutrients for my scalp. And when it finally crumbles away, I'll have built character. Plus, the smell will keep people at a distance, which increases my productivity by at least 15%.

jokes.text: I'm Doctor Cinderella... Cece's really into princesses now, so we decided to turn them into positive female role models. I'm an oncologist.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining her costume choice
jokes.punchline: The specificity of 'oncologist' - cancer doctor princess
jokes.explanation: Smart parenting humor with unexpected specificity. Very Pam to overthink a costume.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm going as a cancer doctor princess. What? It's specific!

jokes.text: And you are a dog. No, I'm a puppy. Dang it! I was worried that would happen.
jokes.setup: Jim's lazy costume attempt
jokes.punchline: Even his minimal effort failed - he's the wrong animal
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim half-effort backfiring. Mild but character-consistent humor.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim, what are you supposed to be?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm a cat.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You're wearing a dog collar.

jokes.text: Sure I am. I am... one of the Men In Black guys.
jokes.setup: Jim claiming he IS in costume
jokes.punchline: His 'costume' is just wearing a suit to work
jokes.explanation: Predictable Jim move but fits character. Low effort matches his approach to Halloween.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I am in costume.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm dressed as a businessman.

jokes.text: Unless he has a secret costume that he told everyone about except for me.
jokes.setup: Pam's ongoing frustration about Jim's business secrecy
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor to something as trivial as Halloween costumes
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends ongoing marital tension to absurd territory. Character-driven.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim won't tell me anything about the business anymore. It's like he's keeping state secrets. Yesterday I asked what he was wearing for Halloween and he said, 'That's classified.'

jokes.text: Getting a lot of mileage out of this, aren't you? Yeah, well, get used to it, bud.
jokes.setup: Jim acknowledging Pam's repeated jabs
jokes.punchline: Pam's unapologetic commitment to staying mad
jokes.explanation: Realistic married couple dynamic. Pam's pettiness is funny and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I get it. You're mad at me.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Mad doesn't even cover it. I'm going to be mad at you forever.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Forever? Come on, Pam.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yes. Forever. I've already blocked out the rest of my calendar.

jokes.text: I could have sworn I saw some Treble somewhere.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if anyone has seen 'Treble'
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'trouble' while introducing his a cappella group
jokes.explanation: Basic pun that sets up the a cappella storyline. Serviceable but not clever.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen Treble?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We're gonna get in a lot of trouble without our fourth member.

jokes.text: What lab did these little clones escape from?
jokes.setup: Seeing the identical-looking a cappella group
jokes.punchline: They all look like laboratory clones
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual observation. The clone imagery works for cookie-cutter college singers.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: They all look like laboratory clones

jokes.text: You went to Cornell? Yeah. Okay, ha, ha, ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Jim's mock surprise about Andy going to Cornell
jokes.punchline: Andy's defensive reaction to the obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Standard Andy insecurity about his obvious characteristics. Functional character beat.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, you went to Cornell? I had no idea.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, I went to Cornell.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's impressive.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, I mean it's impressive that you only mention it like fifty times a day.

jokes.text: Where you boys staying? How does it work in the rooms? You get a privacy partition?
jokes.setup: Packer asking about sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Implying sexual activity between the male singers
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer inappropriate humor. True to character but low-effort gay panic comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: So where are you guys sleeping tonight?

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Both in the same bed? That's cool, that's cool. No judgment.

jokes.text: They might actually call me up to solo on George Michael's Faith. That was one of my signature songs.
jokes.setup: Andy getting excited about potentially performing
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence about his college 'signature song'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for Andy's a cappella delusions. His desperate need for validation shows.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna perform my signature song from college. Oh man, people loved this song.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm talking standing ovations, requests for encores, the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I wrote it myself. It's called 'Sweeney's Song.'

jokes.text: I'm eating Jims. Must eat more Jims.
jokes.setup: Erin eating candy with Jim's name on it
jokes.punchline: Pretending to be a monster eating multiple Jims
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Erin's playful nature. Light humor but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Jim! There's a monster eating you!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Nom nom nom nom! Another Jim! Nom nom nom!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: So many Jims! The monster can't get enough Jims!

jokes.text: Hello, little pill. What do you do?
jokes.setup: Dwight finding a mysterious pill
jokes.punchline: Talking to it like a detective examining evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight treating mundane things dramatically. The direct address to the pill is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What do we have here? A pharmaceutical anomaly. Single tablet, unknown origin, possibly expired.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: The color suggests ibuprofen, but the markings are inconsistent with FDA standards.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm going to need to run a full analysis. This could be anything — a placebo, a performance enhancer, or a threat to Dunder Mifflin's workplace safety protocols.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Nobody touch this. It's evidence now.

jokes.text: Translation, there's a madman in our midst.
jokes.setup: Learning the pill treats anxiety
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to 'madman' conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia and dramatic overreaction. The formal 'translation' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A pill that treats anxiety? That's a sign of weakness. The weak need pills. I don't need pills. I need discipline.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, it's just a—

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Anyone who takes anxiety medication is a madman. A certifiable lunatic.

jokes.text: I'm Sexy Toby. Gross. I love it.
jokes.setup: Toby's costume reveal
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of 'Sexy Toby' and Oscar/Kevin's conflicting reactions
jokes.explanation: 'Sexy Toby' is inherently oxymoronic. The split reactions add to the awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'm going as Sexy Toby.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: That's a contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, nobody wants to see that.

jokes.text: This is a pill that combats insanity, okay? Whoever is taking it is not only insane... they are now off their meds.
jokes.setup: Dwight's investigation into the anxiety medication
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic that having anxiety pills means you're off them
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight logic fail - finding the pill means they're NOT taking it. Flawed reasoning delivered with confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: If you have anxiety pills, that means you're off anxiety. You don't need them anymore.

jokes.text: I once saw him yell at Phyllis for sneezing wrong.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she doesn't want Dwight knowing about her anxiety
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of sneezing 'wrong'
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Dwight's nitpicky nature. The specificity of 'sneezing wrong' is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Nellie
dialogue.line: I don't want Dwight knowing about my anxiety. He'd use it against me. He'd probably make some comment about how I'm sneezing wrong or something equally absurd.

jokes.text: When I got the nickname Boner Champ, that is when I became me.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his college transformation
jokes.punchline: His identity is built around the nickname 'Boner Champ'
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Andy's entire identity hinges on a ridiculous college nickname. Perfectly pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: In college, I was known as 'Boner Champ.' It was my identity. Everything I did, every achievement, every relationship—it all traced back to that one glorious nickname.

jokes.text: Did you say you've got AIDS? No, I don't have AIDS. That's not what I said.
jokes.setup: Andy saying 'You got Qs, I got As'
jokes.punchline: Student mishearing 'As' as 'AIDS'
jokes.explanation: Simple mishearing gag. Gets a laugh but relies on phonetic similarity rather than wit.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You got Qs, I got As.

dialogue.speaker: Cornell Student
dialogue.line: You got AIDS?

jokes.text: You at least want to know why they call me Boner Champ? I thought Broccoli Rob was the Boner Champ.
jokes.setup: Andy proudly offering to explain his nickname
jokes.punchline: Student casually revealing someone else has the title
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Andy's ego. The casual delivery of the devastating news is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You want to know how I got the nickname 'The Nard Dog'?

dialogue.speaker: Cornell student
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, in high school, I was really into acapella, and my friends started calling me 'The Nard Dog' because of my last name, Bernard.

dialogue.speaker: Cornell student
dialogue.line: Oh, that's cool. Yeah, we had a guy at my high school we called 'The Nard Dog' too.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Really? What was his name?

dialogue.speaker: Cornell student
dialogue.line: I don't remember. But he was way cooler than you.

jokes.text: Andy Bernard is the Boner Champ.
jokes.setup: Andy's identity crisis about his nickname
jokes.punchline: His desperate, emphatic declaration of his title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's desperation to maintain his identity. The formal declaration is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm not 'The Nard Dog.' I'm not 'Dwight's friend.' I'm Andrew Bernard, Regional Director of Dunder Mifflin Scranton!

jokes.text: We were thinking somewhere between $5,000, $10,000. I can do the full $10,000. We should just... all in.
jokes.setup: Jim discussing investment amounts with Pam
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to maximum amount without consulting her
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's impulsiveness when trying to fit in. Sets up marital conflict effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So how much should we invest?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe we should start small?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You know what, let's just go all in.

jokes.text: Smear some peanut butter on my forehead. You know, to protect his brain from the nanobots that the government put in the air conditioning.
jokes.setup: Dwight testing if Darryl will agree with crazy behavior
jokes.punchline: Absurd conspiracy theory about government nanobots
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight paranoia with specific absurdity. The peanut butter defense is creative nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Darryl, I need to ask you something important. Do you believe the government is using nanobots to monitor our thoughts?

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: No, Dwight. No, I do not.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, you should. They're in the water supply. I've done my research.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Man, you need help.

jokes.text: I can't really picture it. Can you get it on there? Yeah. And maybe get the cheeks.
jokes.setup: Dwight's crazy peanut butter theory
jokes.punchline: Darryl playing along and encouraging more insanity
jokes.explanation: Darryl's deadpan enablement of Dwight's insanity is funny. Good straight-man work.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Peanut butter is a government conspiracy to control our minds through legume-based mind control particles.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, yeah, I've heard about that. That's real.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You have?

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Yeah, man. They put it in the Skippy. That's the government brand.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Exactly! Thank you. Finally someone who gets it.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: And the creamy kind is stronger than the chunky.

jokes.text: I had sex with a snowman. I just went at that thing. Cold would've stopped most people, but I stayed locked in.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining how he got the Boner Champ nickname
jokes.punchline: Graphic details about having sex with a snowman
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate overshare that's both disturbing and hilarious. 'Stayed locked in' is a perfect awful detail.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I got the nickname 'Boner Champ' because in high school I hooked up with a snowman. And I didn't just... you know... I was very thorough.

jokes.text: And I told Phyllis not to put it out, but she insisted, so anyway...
jokes.setup: Andy's snowman story
jokes.punchline: Implying Phyllis displayed his defiled snowman
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up detail that makes the situation more absurd. Phyllis being oblivious adds to it.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So I built this snowman in my front yard, and someone came along and knocked its head clean off. Just decapitated it.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: That's terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then the next day, I found the head in Phyllis's front window display.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm the electoral college. Ouch! Right on target.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining his costume
jokes.punchline: The senator recognizing it as a political critique
jokes.explanation: Clever political costume that fits Oscar's character. The senator's recognition adds meta-humor.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: I'm dressed as a state senator who takes bribes.

dialogue.speaker: State Senator
dialogue.line: Hey, I know you!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: You do?

dialogue.speaker: State Senator
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're that guy who dresses up like me every Halloween to make fun of me.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I'm just making a general political statement about—

dialogue.speaker: State Senator
dialogue.line: No, no, I get it. It's funny. I'm corrupt. Very clever.

jokes.text: Are... are you me? Yeah. I thought I'd, you know, be you.
jokes.setup: Toby encountering someone dressed as him
jokes.punchline: The awkward realization and Darryl's casual explanation
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment. The simplicity of dressing as a coworker is funny/uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Why are you dressed as me?

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Halloween costume. You're the scariest thing in this office.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: That's... I'm not sure how to feel about that.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Yeah, people are already running away. It's working great.

jokes.text: I have anxiety all the time. Every waking moment of my life is sheer torture.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming he doesn't need anxiety medication
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing symptoms that suggest he desperately needs it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Dwight arguing against anxiety meds while describing crippling anxiety. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I don't need anxiety medication. I have complete control over my emotions.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My heart rate is a steady 140 beats per minute. My palms are sweating through my shirt. I haven't slept in four days, but that's because I'm mentally superior, not anxious.

jokes.text: Cool, free upper. Ha, ha! The jig is up, psychopath!
jokes.setup: Dwight's elaborate trap to catch the pill taker
jokes.punchline: Creed casually taking it as a recreational drug
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - completely wrong about what the pill is but happy to take it. Derails Dwight's plan perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I've set up an elaborate trap to catch whoever's been taking pills from the office supply closet.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Oh nice, are these the good ones?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Don't mind if I do.

jokes.text: Man, by the end, I guess it was... about ten. 'About' ten? Ten. It was the full $10,000.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to downplay his investment
jokes.punchline: Pam catching his attempt to minimize it
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic - Jim trying to soften bad news and Pam not buying it. Realistic tension.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's not a big deal, really. Just a small investment.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim, you literally just told me you mortgaged the house.

jokes.text: That's what she said. What, am I overdoing it?
jokes.setup: Classic 'That's what she said' moment
jokes.punchline: Michael acknowledging he might be overdoing his signature joke
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment where Michael shows self-awareness about his overused joke. Nice callback.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I've been doing that joke for years and it never gets old.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Actually, it does.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: No, do not sing that. Do not... man.
jokes.setup: The group about to perform Andy's 'signature' song
jokes.punchline: Andy's desperate attempt to stop them
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Andy's nightmare unfolds. His powerlessness is funny and painful.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, no, no, wait! That's not my signature song anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have a new signature song now!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Please, just let me sing it for you guys instead!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You're gonna ruin everything!

jokes.text: I thought you were Adam Lambert.
jokes.setup: Andy dressed as George Michael
jokes.punchline: Being mistaken for a different, more recent performer
jokes.explanation: Another blow to Andy's ego - his costume isn't even recognizable. Adds to his humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: Andy Bernard
dialogue.line: Hey, check out my George Michael costume!

dialogue.speaker: Cornell student
dialogue.line: Oh cool, you're dressed as Post Malone?

jokes.text: They said they needed 20 ccs of George Michael, stat, so just... wham! I sprang into action.
jokes.setup: Broccoli Rob explaining why he performed Andy's song
jokes.punchline: Treating music like emergency medicine with the 'wham' pun
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of medical emergency language applied to a cappella. The 'wham' pun is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Broccoli Rob
dialogue.line: I performed Andy's song because music is like emergency medicine. You gotta treat it with a 'wham' — you know, like 'wham' bam thank you ma'am, but also like 'wham' as in impact, urgency. You need that immediate effect or people don't respond.

jokes.text: You pick 12 alums from any year to back you up and I'll do the same, and I'm so confident that I'll win, I won't even warm up.
jokes.setup: Challenge to settle the signature song dispute
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific terms and overconfident posturing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the petty rivalry. The formal challenge structure makes the stakes seem important.

dialogue.speaker: Broccoli Rob
dialogue.line: I'll settle this. My signature song is 'Lazy Scranton.' I wrote it, I perform it, it's mine.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not a real song. You just made that up right now.

dialogue.speaker: Broccoli Rob
dialogue.line: I have witnesses. I have documentation. I have a notarized statement from a guy I know.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Who is this guy?

dialogue.speaker: Broccoli Rob
dialogue.line: His name is Gerald. He's a notary. Also a dentist. Also my cousin. But those facts are separate.

jokes.text: Why don't you ask Trey Anastasio about my pipes? He said, and I quote, 'hey, Rob, nice pipes.'
jokes.setup: Broccoli Rob defending his singing ability
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic nature of the 'compliment' from Phish's lead singer
jokes.explanation: Good deflation - the 'evidence' is incredibly weak for such passionate delivery. Name-drop falls flat.

dialogue.speaker: Broccoli Rob
dialogue.line: I can sing. Trey Anastasio told me I had a beautiful voice.

dialogue.speaker: Trey Anastasio
dialogue.line: You do.

jokes.text: You literally have to? No, I'm just... I'm saying, what would happen if they didn't sing it? Would they go to jail? Would they be shot?
jokes.setup: Jim saying they 'have to' sing Monster Mash
jokes.punchline: Angela taking the phrase literally and escalating to extreme consequences
jokes.explanation: Good Angela pedantry taking casual language literally. Her escalation to violence is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: We have to sing Monster Mash.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: If we have to sing Monster Mash, then we have to do the dance too.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: And if we do the dance, we have to wear the costumes.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: And if we wear the costumes, we have to commit to the bit for the entire workday.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Which means we have to cancel all our meetings, reorganize the office layout, and potentially move to a Halloween-themed workplace indefinitely.

jokes.text: It turns out Pam really, really hates Monster Mash. I mean, like, never bring that song up in front of her.
jokes.setup: The argument appearing to be about logic
jokes.punchline: Revealing Pam just irrationally hates the song
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - what seemed like a philosophical debate was just Pam hating a song. Jim's warning is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But it's a good song though.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: No it's not.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yes it is. Think about it logically—

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I don't care about logic. I hate it.

jokes.text: If I am not Boner Champ... I don't know who I am.
jokes.setup: Andy's identity crisis over his college nickname
jokes.punchline: Treating the loss of 'Boner Champ' as an existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Andy's shallow identity. The dramatic delivery of such a ridiculous statement is gold.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I was Boner Champ in college. That was my nickname. And now... now I'm just Andy. Just regular Andy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Without that name, who am I? What am I? I've lost my identity.

jokes.text: Her name is 'mom.'
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning someone at the Bernard family foundation
jokes.punchline: The obvious revelation delivered as if it's insider information
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment treating the obvious as special knowledge. The formal delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know, I was talking to someone at the Bernard family foundation the other day...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And they told me something pretty wild. Turns out... Bernard is actually a family name.

jokes.text: He's just having a tough time, being wifeless and a high-pressure job and his crazy cousin Mose. Other cousin Mose.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why 'Mose' needs anxiety medication
jokes.punchline: Revealing there are multiple cousins named Mose, one of whom is the problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Dwight logic - multiple Mose cousins, with one causing problems for the other. Perfect Schrute family dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Mose needs anxiety medication because he's very anxious. But here's the thing - there are actually several Moses in my family.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: There's Mose, there's my other cousin Mose, and then there's the Mose who tried to burn down the beet farm. That Mose is the one with the real anxiety problem.

jokes.text: My parents are broke.
jokes.setup: Andy confidently calling his mom about a charitable donation
jokes.punchline: The devastating revelation that ruins his plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. Three words that destroy Andy's entire plan and self-image.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey Mom, I just wanted to let you know I'm donating $5,000 to the Cornell library in your name.

dialogue.speaker: Andy's Mom
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, sweetheart!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I know, right? They're naming the whole east wing after us.

dialogue.speaker: Andy's Mom
dialogue.line: Oh Andy, that's so generous. Your father would be so proud.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well actually, Mom, I was thinking we could make it $10,000 and really make a statement.

dialogue.speaker: Andy's Mom
dialogue.line: Honey, that's so kind of you to think of your Cornell legacy. By the way, did you know your father gave $50,000 to Princeton?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: ...What?

tmdb_overview: It's Halloween in the office and Andy invites his college a cappella group to perform, which leads to a confrontation with his frenemy Broccoli Rob. Meanwhile, Dwight finds evidence that a madman is loose in the office and tries to track him down, and the first meeting of Jim's new job causes a fight with Pam.
insight: A cappella cringe carries mid-tier episode—73 score masks thin joke setup payoff ratio.
